findings.description: The signature/info section layout changed significantly. In the PDF, the NC logo is on the left side with the DATE and form fields arranged in a two-column layout to the right. In the HTML, the fields are stacked vertically in a single column with the logo below them.
findings.evidence: PDF shows DATE on the left with SIGNATURE/PRINTED NAME/TITLE/FACILITY ADDRESS/CITY&ZIP/PHONE/EMAIL fields in a two-column layout beside the NC logo. HTML renders all fields in a single column stacked vertically, with the logo image placed below the fields.

findings.description: The NC Waste Management logo is rendered as a black rectangle in the HTML instead of showing the actual NC logo graphic.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC Waste Management Environmental Quality logo clearly. HTML shows a solid black rectangle placeholder instead of the logo.

findings.description: The footer content appears both in the main footer element and partially duplicated - the inspector map URL and contact info appear at the bottom footer and also referenced in the page-header area of the original PDF. However, in the HTML the footer text and the top-of-document references are both present, which matches the PDF's own structure (top and bottom references).
findings.evidence: PDF has the DSCA inspector map link and contact note at both top and bottom of the page. HTML preserves both, which is faithful.

findings.description: The subtitle 'Voluntary Self-inspection Checklist for Petroleum and Alternative-solvent Dry Cleaners' is rendered as h2 which is reasonable, but both the h1 and h2 are placed inside a 'page-header' classed header element. The hierarchy is acceptable and not broken.
findings.evidence: h1: 'DRY-CLEANING SOLVENT CLEANUP ACT (DSCA)', h2: 'Voluntary Self-inspection Checklist...'

findings.description: The HTML has two nested <main> elements, which is a structural duplication issue, though this is a DOM structure problem rather than content duplication.
